graph_vector_fulltext.message: An AI system developed, placed on the market, put into service, or used for military, defence, or national security purposes is excluded from the scope of this Regulation. It falls back within scope if it is used outside those purposes—temporarily or permanently—for other purposes, such as civilian or humanitarian purposes, law enforcement, or public security. In that case, the entity using the system for the non-military/defence/national-security purpose must ensure the system complies with the Regulation, unless it is already compliant. Also, if a system is placed on the market or put into service for both an excluded purpose (military/defence/national security) and one or more non-excluded purposes (e.g., civilian or law enforcement), it is within scope and the provider must ensure compliance.
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_question: Under what circumstances does an AI system developed for military purposes fall back within the scope of this Regulation?
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_answer: An AI system developed, placed on the market, put into service, or used for military, defence, or national security purposes is excluded from the scope of this Regulation. It falls back within scope if it is used outside those purposes—temporarily or permanently—for other purposes, such as civilian or humanitarian purposes, law enforcement, or public security. In that case, the entity using the system for the non-military/defence/national-security purpose must ensure the system complies with the Regulation, unless it is already compliant. Also, if a system is placed on the market or put into service for both an excluded purpose (military/defence/national security) and one or more non-excluded purposes (e.g., civilian or law enforcement), it is within scope and the provider must ensure compliance.
